title: Madame Georges Charpentier (Marguerite-Louise Lemonnier, 1848–1904) and Her Children, Georgette-Berthe (1872–1945) and Paul-Emile-Charles (1875–1895)
summary: Renoir's 1878 portrait of Madame Charpentier and her children, showcasing domestic elegance and Impressionist brushwork.

title: The Chess Players
summary: A tempera painting from 1475 depicting figures engaged in a game of chess.

title: Scene from a Novella
summary: A tempera painting on wood by Liberale da Verona depicting a narrative scene from a novella.

title: The Birth of the Virgin
summary: Fra Carnevale's 1467 tempera and oil panel depicting the Virgin Mary's birth with architectural detail.

title: Tommaso di Folco Portinari (1428–1501); Maria Portinari (Maria Maddalena Baroncelli, born 1456)
summary: Hans Memling's paired portraits of a Florentine banker and his young wife, painted in oil on wood around 1470.

title: The Last Communion of Saint Jerome
summary: Botticelli depicts Saint Jerome's final communion with delicate tempera and gold on wood.

title: An Egyptian Peasant Woman and Her Child
summary: Bonnat depicts an Egyptian mother and child with realistic detail, reflecting 19th-century European Orientalist interest.

title: Virgin and Child with Four Angels
summary: Gerard David's devotional panel depicting Mary and Christ child surrounded by angels, exemplifying Northern Renaissance detail and piety.

title: Virgin and Child Enthroned
summary: Bohemian Gothic panel painting of the Virgin and Child with gold leaf, circa 1345-1350.

title: The Crucifixion
summary: Gerard David's oil painting depicts Christ's crucifixion with detailed figures and landscape, created around 1495.
